data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-e543f054">
    <title>Provider status must consume Fizeau status DTOs directly</title>
    <description>
DDx provider/status surfaces still translate Fizeau HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo into DDx-local RoutingSignalSnapshot/read-model fields in cli/internal/server/providers.go and cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go. That keeps provider quota, auth, freshness, usage, cost class, request-fit, and default-model interpretation in DDx instead of treating Fizeau as the routing/status authority. Replace DDx-local signal derivation with direct consumption of Fizeau public status DTOs, or add the missing DTO fields upstream first and then consume them. In-scope files: cli/internal/server/providers.go, cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go, frontend/provider GraphQL models if field shape changes, tests. Out-of-scope: changing routing decisions or provider probing behavior inside Fizeau.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/server/providers.go no longer has signalFromHarnessInfo, providerQuotaHeadroomStr, providerAuthStateStr, harnessDefaultModels, harnessCosCostClassStr, or DDx-local default-model guesses for Fizeau providers. 2. cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go no longer reconstructs provider quota/auth/cost semantics from HarnessInfo when equivalent Fizeau DTO fields exist. 3. Any missing Fizeau field required by the UI is tracked upstream, not reimplemented in DDx. 4. Provider UI/API tests assert fields come from Fizeau ProviderInfo/HarnessInfo DTO values, not DDx heuristics. 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:server, area:providers, area:routing, kind:cleanup, triage:needs-investigation</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="7cc9bfe89af8f402f4d590cfb1a4ff620d864763" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T080913-e4877f7c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `6d69dc6` with message `refactor: consume harness dto fields directly [ddx-e543f054]`.

What changed:
- [cli/internal/server/providers.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/providers.go)
  - Removed the DDx-local `RoutingSignalSnapshot` shim and the old default-model/quota/auth/cost helpers.
  - Switched provider summary/detail assembly to read `HarnessInfo` fields directly.
  - Built `models`, `auth_state`, `quota_headroom`, `signal_sources`, `freshness_ts`, and `request_fit` from upstream DTO data.
- [cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go)
  - `HarnessStatuses` now maps directly from `HarnessInfo` via a new helper instead of the rate-limit cache bridge.
- [cli/internal/server/providers_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/providers_test.go)
  - Updated to assert direct DTO-driven values and removed the old synthetic signal-source enum check.
- [cli/internal/server/graphql/providers_unified_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/graphql/providers_unified_test.go)
  - Added coverage for the new direct harness-status mapping.

Validation:
- `go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -short -race ./internal/server/...`

I also built `cli/build/ddx` locally so the `cmd` acceptance tests could run, but I did not commit generated build artifacts.
